experiences.intentions: Two 2-tiles are at positions (1,2) and (2,1) with many empty spaces, so there is no immediate threat of losing. The goal is to combine tiles efficiently while keeping the board organized. Moving down will shift the upper 2 down into the same column as the lower area and place both tiles on the bottom row adjacent at columns 1 and 2, setting up an immediate merge with a following left or right move while keeping tiles consolidated near an edge. Up/left/right also move tiles but do not create as clean a next-step merge setup.
experiences.tasks: Achieve the highest possible tile in 2048 by merging tiles strategically.

Candidate actions: down keeps the strongest corner anchored and compresses tiles downward, likely merging the two 2s in the right columns into a 4 while preserving the left stack; left also preserves the corner but causes less useful consolidation right now; up would pull the 32 out of the corner, weakening the setup; right definitely breaks corner anchoring. Best sub-goal now is maintain bottom-left corner and seek safe merges, so down is best.
experiences.tasks: Achieve the highest possible tile in 2048 by merging tiles strategically.
